name: wilson-pool
description: Route heavy Bash commands to a remote host roster via ssh (PreToolUse updatedInput). Each host carries a platform tag (macOS-only / Linux-only commands go to a matching host, else round-robined) and an optional per-host workdir override. workdir may be an explicit path or `auto` (mirror the current project — local ~/<rel> → remote ~/<rel>). `autosync on` rsyncs the local project to the target host before each routed command — a continuously-changing tree stays fresh with zero manual sync, and the workdir is created if absent (additive by default so remote build caches survive; `autosync mirror` adds rsync --delete). With `autosync off`, a pre-flight `test -d` check skips routing to any host that lacks the workdir (no more `cd: no such file`). Spirit-port of wilson's pool roster. Routing is OFF until the roster has >=1 host AND workdir is set; only the Bash tool is routed.
